title: jwt.sign with allowInsecureKeySizes: true — signs tokens with a key below the RSA minimum, weakening every signature

params.passDetail: jwt.sign does not force allowInsecureKeySizes: true — the library enforces its minimum key-size check, so tokens are signed with an adequately sized key.
params.failDetail: jwt.sign is called with allowInsecureKeySizes: true, which suppresses jsonwebtoken's built-in guard that rejects RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits. Tokens then get signed with an undersized (brute-forceable) key and the signatures they carry are weak — an attacker who factors the small key can forge valid tokens. Remove the flag (or set it false) so the minimum-key-size check protects every signature.
params.absentCallDetail: No jwt.sign call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: jwt.sign does not pass an allowInsecureKeySizes literal; the library default (false, key-size check enforced) applies.

provenance.note: Copied verbatim from the bezkoder/node-js-jwt-auth tutorial, whose login controller signs with { algorithm: 'HS256', allowInsecureKeySizes: true }. The flag exists only to bypass jsonwebtoken's post-9.0 minimum-key-size guard; a starter that ships it teaches agents to weaken every signature to silence a warning.
